sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: Founder at Smol.ai, AI Researcher
sources.post_summary: Discusses the evolution of AI agents from hype to practical deployment, highlighting key frameworks like LangChain and AutoGPT for building autonomous systems. Shares insights on challenges in reliability and scaling agentic workflows. Emphasizes the need for better developer tools to streamline agent orchestration.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder at Eureka Labs, ex-OpenAI
sources.post_summary: Explores advancements in AI infrastructure for training large models, focusing on efficient compute scaling and new hardware like TPUs. Predicts how this will accelerate agent-based automation in software development. Calls for open-source tools to democratize access.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-founder at GitHub, Investor
sources.post_summary: Highlights emerging developer tools powered by AI agents that automate code review and testing pipelines. Shares examples from recent integrations in VS Code extensions. Stresses impact on productivity for engineering teams.

sources.author: Eliot Andres
sources.headline: AI Engineer at Replicate
sources.post_summary: Breaks down building production-grade AI agents with custom infrastructure stacks, including vector DBs and orchestration layers. Provides tips on monitoring and debugging agent failures. Discusses trade-offs in latency vs. capability.

sources.author: Hamel Husain
sources.headline: Independent AI Consultant, ex-GitHub
sources.post_summary: Analyzes the state of automation in dev tools via AI, critiquing current agent limitations and proposing hybrid human-AI loops. Recommends specific open-source libraries for robust pipelines. Forecasts rapid iteration in the next year.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ Overloop | Helping RevOps & Sales leaders scale outbound with automation
sources.post_summary: Discusses building a GTM engineering function from scratch, focusing on integrating outbound automation tools to boost pipeline efficiency. Shares a framework for sales ops teams to automate prospecting without losing personalization. Emphasizes measurable ROI in revenue operations.

sources.author: Amy Volas
sources.headline: CEO @ Avenue Talent Partners | Sales & RevOps Fractional Leader
sources.post_summary: Outlines a content strategy playbook for revenue operations teams to align marketing and sales. Highlights using data-driven content to support outbound efforts and GTM motions. Provides templates for sales ops to repurpose content effectively.

sources.author: Kyle Coleman
sources.headline: Head of Sales @ Salesloft | GTM Strategy Expert
sources.post_summary: Explains how sales ops can leverage outbound automation to scale GTM without increasing headcount. Details tech stack recommendations and pitfalls in revenue operations implementation. Shares case study with 3x pipeline growth.

sources.author: Dan Tyre
sources.headline: VP of Sales @ HubSpot | Revenue Operations Advocate
sources.post_summary: Breaks down evolving role of sales ops in modern GTM, integrating automation and content syndication. Advises on building cross-functional revenue operations teams for outbound success. Includes actionable steps for content strategy alignment.

sources.author: Belkins.io Team
sources.headline: Outbound Sales & Lead Generation Agency | GTM Specialists
sources.post_summary: Case study on automating outbound for B2B SaaS using custom GTM engineering workflows. Covers sales ops optimizations and content strategies that drove 40% revenue uplift. Focuses on scalable revenue operations playbooks.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK, and other bootstrapped products
sources.post_summary: Pieter shares his journey as a solo founder building and shipping multiple products like Nomad List without VC funding. He emphasizes the power of building in public, iterating based on user feedback, and achieving profitability quickly. His post details the tech stack and lessons from launching 12 startups in 12 months.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Serial Entrepreneur, Author of Zero to Sold
sources.post_summary: Arvid recounts pivoting from corporate job to full-time indie hacker, building and selling his first SaaS product FeedbackPanda for $200k+. He advises on validating ideas quickly and building in public to gain early users and feedback. The post includes a step-by-step guide for others considering a tech career pivot.

sources.author: KP (Karthik Pattabiraman)
sources.headline: Solo Founder at SuperHi
sources.post_summary: KP details shipping his no-code web design tool SuperHi as a solo developer, from idea to 10k users. He shares transparent revenue numbers, challenges faced, and tips for solo builders on focus and marketing. Encourages others to ship imperfect products and iterate publicly.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO at Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil reflects on his career pivot from Pinterest to bootstrapping Gumroad, now a multi-million ARR product. He discusses building in public since day one, the importance of creator economy tools, and lessons on sustainable growth without investors. Post includes metrics and future plans.

sources.author: Courtland Allen
sources.headline: Founder of Indie Hackers
sources.post_summary: Courtland shares stories of solo devs and founders shipping products via his platform, including his own pivot into tech entrepreneurship. Highlights community-driven building in public, with examples of bootstrapped successes and common pitfalls to avoid in product launches.
